summary: Iteration 164: end-to-end regression for the new inline 'open balance' badge + receivables-summary backend extension. Backend pytest (8 tests) confirms oldest_overdue_due_date field is returned as ISO YYYY-MM-DD or null, all existing fields preserved, value is null when overdue_balance==0, non-null when overdue_balance>0, and always strictly < today. Frontend: 26 balance badges on Sales History (walk-in rows have 0 badges as required), 39 on Reports AR Aging tab, 89 on Accounting Receivables tab. Color tones verified (amber=17 / red=9 on Sales). Cache de-dup verified: across SPA navigation Sales -> Reports -> Accounting -> Sales the /api/customers/receivables-summary endpoint is hit exactly ONCE. Click-through regression: customer name still opens 'Statement of Account — <Name>' Dialog on all three pages. Zero console errors related to badge component.

backend_pytest.summary: 7 passed, 1 skipped (test_null_when_no_overdue skipped because all 39 test customers have overdue balances)

checks.existing_fields_preserved: PASS (id, name, balance, overdue_balance, invoice_count, overdue_count, interest_rate, grace_period, credit_limit)

sample_badge_titles: Open ₱109,149.00 · ₱107,349.00 overdue (25d past due, oldest due 2026-03-31)
Open ₱18,423.00 · ₱18,000.00 overdue (38d past due, oldest due 2026-03-18)
Open ₱4,047.35 · ₱4,043.47 overdue (58d past due, oldest due 2026-02-26)

cache_dedup.verdict: PASS — single fetch shared across all three pages within 30s window via module-level _cache + _inFlight de-dup

test_results.console_errors: 0 errors related to balance badge or receivables-summary

critical_code_review_comments: CustomerBalanceBadge.js correctly hides itself when row not found, balance ≤ 0.005, or customerId is falsy (covers walk-in case implicitly). Verified at runtime: 16 walk-in rows on Sales rendered 0 badges.

Cache implementation in useCustomerBalances() correctly de-duplicates concurrent fetches via _inFlight promise; module-level _cache survives SPA route changes. Single network call confirmed across 3 distinct pages.
Three pages wire byId via { byId: balanceById } = useCustomerBalances() and pass <CustomerBalanceBadge customerId={...} byId={balanceById} /> consistently. No prop-drilling duplication.
Backend $min aggregation with $cond: ['$is_overdue', '$due_date', None] correctly returns null when no overdue invoices and the earliest due_date when present. Verified 7 pytest assertions.

Note (pre-existing, unrelated to this change): Statement of Account modal uses native browser date inputs (mm/dd/yyyy + 04/25/2026) instead of shadcn Calendar. Not a regression — flagging only because system prompt asks design reviewers to mention it. Not in scope of current task.
seed_data_creation: None — read-only verification against existing 39 customers with non-zero balances.
context_for_next_testing_agent: When testing the Accounting Receivables tab, use page.get_by_role('tab', name='Receivables', exact=True) NOT text=Receivables — the latter may match a heading/card label instead of the tab and leave the Expenses tab active (false negative on badge count). Same caveat applies to Reports AR Aging — though in practice text=AR Aging works there because the only match is the tab. The cache test needs SPA navigation (history.pushState + popstate dispatch) — page.goto() does a hard reload and resets _cache/_cacheTime module state, which would falsely show 3 calls.
rca of the issue: No issues found. Feature implementation is correct end-to-end.
